intro: Cladocolea grahamii, also known as Graham's mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species belonging to the family Loranthaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Cladocolea grahamii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cladocolea grahamii is often used in traditional medicine and can be found in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
